comment: Flow_BoxBlobPurge (Phase 7 / ADR-0012 / build-plan 04 §11, Wave 5 / box-flow-patterns Fragment 5). SCHEDULED, status-driven Blob cleanup. Box is the archive of record; the Azure Blob is the transient working store. Purge is STATUS-DRIVEN, never a blind age rule — a Blob lifecycle policy cannot read Dataverse status, so the flow (which re-checks Dataverse) is primary; a tag-filtered lifecycle rule with soft-delete on is the cheap backstop. Deletes ONLY the transient Blob evidence bytes for cases that have reached box_synced (terminal) + a grace window; it NEVER deletes the Box copy (the archive of record) and never the cr1bd_evidence row — it clears the storagepath reference. HOUSE RULES: gate cr1bd_BOX_API_ENABLED is READ not defined (default OFF — purge is only relevant once Box is the archive); first-party shared_azureblob DeleteFile_V2 only (no shared_box_rest op anywhere here — bytes/cleanup are first-party). DATAVERSE DEPENDENCY: the purge filter keys on cr1bd_status eq 100000009 (box_synced) AND cr1bd_boxsyncedat lt now-grace; cr1bd_boxsyncedat is the build-plan-04 flow-contract column that dataverse-data-architect must declare on cr1bd_case before activation. LINTER: the cr1bd_cases ListRecords here (status+boxsyncedat filter) is a DOCUMENTED exception in validate-flows.mjs (it is not a dedup-by-VRM read). [BUILD] state=off. ACTIVATION [RESERVED-FOR-USER]: set PurgeGraceDays (operator pin 7/30/60), bind cr1bd_evidenceblob, flip BOX_API_ENABLED in a test env, confirm the Recurrence startTime is in the future so it does not fire on deploy. Claude never flips the gate or binds the connection.

metadata.description: Days after box_synced before the transient Blob bytes are purged (the Box archive copy is retained). Default 7; operator may pin 7/30/60.

Recurrence.comment: Daily. startTime is a far-future placeholder so the flow NEVER fires on import/deploy (Logic Apps Recurrence fires immediately if no startTime is set). The operator sets a real near-term startTime at activation (e.g. an off-peak hour). A polling trigger reprocesses pending occurrences on re-enable.

Get_gate_definition.comment: READ the gate (never DEFINE it). Default OFF -> the whole purge is skipped.

Init_cutoff.comment: The grace boundary: cases whose cr1bd_boxsyncedat is older than (now - PurgeGraceDays) are eligible. mul(...,-1) makes the day count negative for addDays.

If_gate_on.comment: Gate-on path only. Gate-off -> no-op (no Blob touched).

List_purgeable_cases.comment: DOCUMENTED LINTER EXCEPTION (status+boxsyncedat, NOT a dedup-by-VRM read). Cases that reached box_synced(100000009, terminal) AND were synced longer ago than the grace window. box_synced is terminal so a purged case never re-enters the pipeline.

parameters.$filter: _cr1bd_caseid_value eq @{item()?['cr1bd_caseid']} and cr1bd_storagepath ne null and cr1bd_kind eq 100000000 and cr1bd_acceptedforeva eq true and cr1bd_excluded eq false

List_case_blob_evidence.comment: Purges ONLY the evidence finalize archived to Box: accepted, non-excluded IMAGES (cr1bd_kind eq 100000000 and cr1bd_acceptedforeva eq true and cr1bd_excluded eq false) that still reference Blob bytes (non-empty cr1bd_storagepath). finalize-eva-box uploads exactly this set to Box, so these are safe to purge (the Box copy is the archive of record). Non-archived evidence (instructions / .eml / valuation / rejected images) Blob bytes are intentionally RETAINED here — full-artifact archival to Box + their subsequent purge is a deferred follow-up; purging them by storagepath-only would be permanent data loss of bytes never archived. _cr1bd_caseid_value is the lookup foreign key.

Resolve_blob_id.comment: cr1bd_storagepath holds the blob PATH (classify-persist stamps it from CreateFile_V2's BlobMetadata 'Path'; finalize-eva-box reads it with the PATH-typed GetFileContentByPath_V2). DeleteFile_V2 is keyed on the connector's opaque 'id', NOT the path — BlobMetadata exposes Path and Id as distinct fields. So resolve the id from the path first via the PATH-typed metadata op, then delete by that id. A blob that is already gone makes this 404/Fail -> the delete is skipped but Clear_storagepath still runs (the pointer is dead either way). dataset='AccountNameFromSettings' (access-key auth, same as CreateFile_V2/GetFileContentByPath_V2).

Delete_blob.comment: FIRST-PARTY Azure Blob DeleteFile_V2 (params dataset + id; non-V2 DeleteFile is DEPRECATED). The 'id' is the connector's opaque blob identifier resolved by Resolve_blob_id (BlobMetadata.Id), NOT the stored path — feeding the PATH into 'id' fails/404s. Deletes ONLY the transient Blob copy. NEVER the Box copy (no shared_box_rest / shared_box op here). Only runs when the id resolved (Succeeded); an already-gone blob skips here and is cleaned up by Clear_storagepath.

Clear_storagepath.comment: Clear the storagepath reference (the row + its Box archive metadata are RETAINED; only the dead Blob pointer is cleared) ONLY when the blob was actually deleted (Succeeded) or was already gone (Skipped = Resolve_blob_id 404'd, so Delete_blob never ran). 'Failed' is deliberately DROPPED: on a genuine transient Delete_blob failure (429/5xx) the blob still EXISTS, so clearing the pointer would orphan the blob AND write a false 'purged' audit. Leaving the pointer set on Failed means the next scheduled purge run re-lists this evidence and retries the delete.

Apply_to_each_evidence.comment: Sequential per-evidence (concurrency 1) so a partial failure is deterministic and re-runnable; failure-isolated (the case-level audit still records).

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('purged ', string(length(coalesce(outputs('List_case_blob_evidence')?['body/value'], json('[]')))), ' transient Blob evidence object(s) for case ', item()?['cr1bd_caseid'], ' (box_synced + ', string(parameters('PurgeGraceDays')), 'd grace); Box archive copy retained')

Audit_blob_purged_case.comment: Audit the per-case purge. Reuses action box_synced=100000016 (Info) with explicit blob_purged semantics in name/after — the SAME reuse precedent as intake's casepo_assigned on 100000003; no new audit value is minted by this flow (the dataverse choiceset owns the vocabulary). runAfter the evidence loop on ALL outcomes so the audit always records.

Apply_to_each_case.comment: Per purgeable case: list its Blob-backed evidence -> delete each Blob + clear the pointer -> audit. Modest concurrency (4) across cases.
